ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for low-carb gyudon combo. FOODON_00002764 (beef dish) used as the closest defensible parent class. The dish is a Japanese-style beef bowl variant served with salad and tofu instead of rice.
ontology.foodex2_description: Beef, cooked — used as the closest FoodEx2 anchor for the primary protein component (simmered beef) in this mixed Japanese set meal. Full composite dish code not available in FoodEx2.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for low-carb gyudon combo. A0FJP (cooked beef) used as the primary ingredient anchor. Composite dish structure not representable in a single FoodEx2 code.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for gyudon or low-carb Japanese beef bowl combo. SNOMED CT does not maintain granular codes for restaurant-style composite Asian dishes.

main_ingredients: gyudon sauce (soy sauce, mirin, dashi, sugar)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard set meal portion of approximately 450 g, comprising simmered beef slices over salad and firm tofu, a half-boiled egg, and a choice of miso soup or a drink.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — SR Legacy and Foundation Foods composite estimate for Japanese-style beef bowl with tofu, egg, and salad
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA FoodData Central entry exists for low-carb gyudon combo. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries for: beef (simmered, lean), firm tofu, mixed salad greens, hard-boiled egg, soy sauce, and miso soup. The input record's per_100g values (energy 106.67 kcal, protein 7.78 g, fat 6.22 g, carbohydrate 5.56 g) are consistent with this composite and are retained with medium confidence. Sodium at 333.3 mg/100g is plausible but likely conservative given soy sauce and miso contributions. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, and several micronutrients are set to -1 where USDA composite data for this specific preparation is not reliably available.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition; analogous estimate based on mixed beef and tofu dish with low carbohydrate density
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for low-carb gyudon combo. GI estimated at approximately 32 (low) based on analogous mixed protein-dominant dishes with low carbohydrate content from Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. The dish replaces rice with salad and tofu, substantially reducing carbohydrate load. GL per serving calculated as: GI (32) × carbohydrate per serving (31.5 g from 450 g × 5.56 g/100g) / 100 = 10.1. This is consistent with a low-GI, moderate-GL profile for a protein-rich, low-carbohydrate Japanese set meal.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium from soy sauce, miso soup, and gyudon sauce; reported 333 mg sodium/100g likely underestimates real-world intake
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced soy sauce and miso soup, ask for gyudon sauce on the side, and be aware that actual sodium per serving (450 g) may exceed 1500 mg — well above the 600 mg per-meal target recommended by clinical hypertension guidelines.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate content (5.56 g/100g; ~25 g per serving) with high protein from beef, tofu, and egg moderating postprandial glucose response
type2_diabetes.guidance: This low-carb gyudon variant is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its low carbohydrate density and high protein content; however, patients should request reduced gyudon sauce to minimise added sugar from mirin and should monitor portion size if consuming the optional drink.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (2.22 g/100g; ~10 g per serving) from beef and trans fat (0.11 g/100g) present; high sodium from condiments also elevates cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion frequency due to saturated fat and sodium content; choosing the drink option over miso soup reduces sodium load, and requesting leaner beef cuts or additional tofu reduces saturated fat intake per serving.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Beef is rich in arachidonic acid (omega-6), which amplifies TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory pathways directly relevant to RA
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should consider substituting beef with chicken, fish, or additional tofu; miso soup and tofu provide beneficial fermented-food anti-inflammatory properties but miso adds significant sodium.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Low Carb Gyudon Combo is a Japanese-style set meal adapted for low-carbohydrate eating, served at Japanese fast-food restaurants in Singapore. The dish features thinly sliced beef simmered in a savoury gyudon sauce made from soy sauce, mirin, dashi stock, and a small amount of sugar, served over a bed of fresh mixed salad greens and firm tofu instead of the traditional steamed rice base. The set is accompanied by a half-boiled egg and a choice of miso soup or a beverage. The flavour profile is umami-rich and savoury from the soy-based sauce, with a soft, silky texture from the simmered beef contrasting with the crisp freshness of the salad and the firm, neutral tofu. Nutritionally, the dish provides approximately 107 kcal per 100 g, with 7.8 g protein, 6.2 g fat, and only 5.6 g carbohydrate per 100 g — making it significantly lower in carbohydrates than a standard gyudon rice bowl. A full 450 g serving delivers approximately 480 kcal, 35 g protein, 28 g fat, and 25 g carbohydrate. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 32 (low), with a glycemic load of approximately 10 per serving. The dish is clinically relevant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes due to its low carbohydrate density and high protein content, which moderates postprandial glucose response. However, sodium content is a concern for hypertension management, as soy sauce, miso soup, and gyudon sauce collectively contribute significant sodium that may exceed 1500 mg per serving. Saturated fat from beef (approximately 10 g per serving) is a moderate cardiovascular concern. The dish contains soy, egg, wheat (from soy sauce), and gluten allergens. It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or halal-certified diets without modification.
common_user_queries: Is low carb gyudon good for diabetes?
How many calories in a gyudon set meal without rice?
Is gyudon with tofu low carb?
Low carb Japanese beef bowl Singapore
Gyudon salad tofu egg set meal nutrition
Is gyudon combo suitable for keto?
How much sodium in gyudon set meal?
Japanese beef bowl without rice nutrition

confidence_reason: No single approved source entry exists for low-carb gyudon combo as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from a USDA FoodData Central composite of individual ingredients (simmered beef, firm tofu, mixed salad, half-boiled egg, soy sauce, miso). The input record's per_100g values are consistent with this composite and are retained. GI is an analogous estimate from peer-reviewed international GI tables. Confidence is medium rather than high because exact dish-level data from an approved primary source is unavailable.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, full micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific low-carb gyudon composite dish are not available as a single entry in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2024. Values would require laboratory analysis of the specific restaurant preparation. 2. Sodium at 333.3 mg/100g is likely conservative; real-world sodium from soy sauce, miso soup, and gyudon sauce may be substantially higher — vendor verification recommended. 3. The input record's health_suitability entry for rheumatoid_arthritis referenced kimchi, which is not a component of this dish; this reference has been corrected and removed. 4. Two duplicate portion entries (both 450 g, both is_default: true) were present in the input; consolidated to a single 450 g default serving. 5. GI estimated at 32 (low) based on analogous protein-dominant, low-carbohydrate mixed dish from Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables; no direct GI measurement for this dish exists in approved sources. 6. All core macros (protein_g 7.78, fat_g 6.22, carbohydrate_g 5.56) are non-zero and consistent with the described dish composition; no zero-macro justification required. 7. This dish is sold in Singapore as a Japanese fast-food adaptation; multilingual names in Chinese (低碳水牛丼套餐), Malay (Kombo Gyudon Rendah Karbohidrat), Tamil (குறைந்த கார்போஹைட்ரேட் கியுடான் கொம்போ), and Hindi (लो कार्ब ग्युडोन कॉम्बो) have been added to support Singapore market multilingual retrieval, in addition to the mandatory Japanese name (低糖質牛丼コンボ). These are descriptive transliterations/translations of the English name into each language.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built full RESULT JSON from input. Identified that input health_suitability referenced kimchi (not present in dish) — corrected. Consolidated duplicate portion entries. Added Japanese local name and romanization. Added full ontology with honest gap notes. Added GI from peer-reviewed analogous estimate. Added all three required health_context conditions including Type 2 Diabetes. Populated all -1 fields with documentation in notes. Verified aliases consistency between aliases_by_language and flat aliases array.
Iteration 2: Corrected ontology match_type fields to 'closest_parent'. Re-evaluated multilingual naming compliance for Japanese cuisine.

Iteration 3: Addressed validator G6 failure — added Chinese simplified (低碳水牛丼套餐), Chinese traditional (低碳水牛丼套餐), Malay (Kombo Gyudon Rendah Karbohidrat), Tamil (குறைந்த கார்போஹைட்ரேட் கியுடான் கொம்போ), and Hindi (लो कार्ब ग्युடோन कॉम्बो) local names to local_names section. Added all five new local-language strings to flat aliases array and to aliases_by_language under their respective language keys. This resolves G6, Q1, and Q8 failures reported by the validator. All 13 aliases in aliases_by_language verified to appear verbatim in flat aliases array.
